meta.subject: Full Rust rewrite of the rac-core engine (native-engine-spike), byte-parity-gated against the frozen Python oracle, derived-index cache deleted by design
meta.before_ref: Python oracle: src/ at origin/main 1e0e2c7 (re-pinned; byte-identical src/ to original pin 21c8be4), .venv-oracle rac 0.1.dev50+g21c8be403; plus oracle-next at retrieval branch f2091be (0.1.dev55+gf2091befd) for the retrieve surface
meta.select_how: two binaries, identical argv/cwd/env via the parity harness seam (piped stdio, neutralized telemetry, RAC_NO_CACHE=1)
meta.measured_at: 2026-07-11T20:45Z re-pin re-measurement (startup/single-file/live corpus, 423 artifacts); synthetic matrix from 16:31Z final run (engine delta since is additive only)

correctness_gate.examiner: 174-case byte-parity suite across two oracles (130 mainline + 44 retrieve) + oracle-generated vector suites
correctness_gate.result: 130/130 vs mainline oracle AND 44/44 retrieve cases vs the retrieval-branch oracle, each run twice, scoreboards byte-identical, independently verified
correctness_gate.examiner_not_bent: git diff origin/main...HEAD -- tests/ src/ is empty; parity-cases.json changes vs origin/main audited additive-only

fuzz.honest_finding: the fuzzer's first real catch was a bug in the rewrite (i64 overflow seam), and 30+ minimized inputs crash the PYTHON oracle uncaught while the rewrite reports a normal finding

correctness_gate.retrieve_surface: rac retrieve (compound grounding retrieval: Applies-To scope binding, supersedes resolution, budget truncation) ported ahead of its merge; every command the six-tool Lore MCP surface depends on now has a parity-proven Rust implementation
correctness_gate.mcp_surface: native rust MCP server (rust/rac-mcp): six tools, wire parity 56/56 vs mainline python server and 76/76 vs retrieval-branch server (byte-equal frames, twice each, adversarially verified); serving startup-to-first-result 2206ms->35ms, get_summary 1973ms->39ms, retrieve_grounding 1117ms->172ms
correctness_gate.oracle_defect_found: python cache-on dedupes df, no-cache double-counts duplicate query tokens: warm vs cold python return different ranking-evidence bytes, violating ADR-112's byte-identical invariant (PORT-CONTRACT.d/10 section 0a); rust matches no-cache; upstream fix needed

live_corpus_python_warm_cache.note: rust has NO cache; python number is its best cached path

scale_curve_files_per_s.note: after-line flat (scale-invariant) across 1k-20k; before-line flat but 45-52x lower; ADR-107 recorded python OOM at 15.9 GiB at 1M pre-streaming

million_file_projection.note: ADR-107 budget line; before = 18.8 min recorded, after = 1e6 / 34697

memory.note: getrusage(children); final-run identical readings likely a shared measurement artifact; either reading is far under the 1 GiB target
memory.python_scale_wall: pre-streaming build OOM at 15.9 GiB at 1M files (ADR-107)

cache_deletion.python_cache_stack: ADR-099/103/104/105/106/107/108/112: mmap index store, event-sourced freshness, stat-proxy scans, parallel cold build
cache_deletion.claim: the rewrite beats python's best cached path 8x while deleting the entire cache stack

structure_loc.note: cross-language; counts via wc -l on .rs vs .py, engine scope only, not directly comparable line-for-line
structure_loc.rust_loc_breakdown: the YAML-1.1 SafeLoader port (scanner/parser/composer/resolver) is ~4k lines alone; python gets it free from PyYAML
structure_loc.rewrite_authenticity: total by construction (different language); the meaningful fidelity axis is semantic: the 130-case byte-parity suite + ~10 MB of oracle-generated vector suites

by_design_sidecar: MCP HTTP transport (stdio ported; ADR-098 http pending)
not_solved.oracle_crashes: 30+ minimized inputs crash the python engine uncaught; the rewrite intentionally reports instead of crashing (documented divergence class)
not_solved.amdahl_20k: synthetic 20k scales only 1.6x over sequential (render + OKF residual); parse-dominated real corpora scale 2.8x on 4 cores
not_solved.mcp_status: native stdio MCP server ported with wire parity (56/56 + 76/76); remaining: HTTP transport, audit/telemetry sidecars behind documented seam
